knownIssues.title: Bionic display — dual-panel system with perceptible seam
knownIssues.description: The XR-3 uses two panels per eye: a high-density focus area (1920×1920) inside a lower-density periphery panel. In static images users can sometimes perceive the transition between the two zones.
knownIssues.workaround: This is an inherent design tradeoff. The seam is largely invisible in motion; prioritize the foveated rendering settings over fighting the seam.

knownIssues.title: Mixed-reality passthrough requires enterprise license
knownIssues.description: The XR-3's video passthrough for MR/XR is gated behind a paid enterprise license. Consumer VR features work without it, but the XR functionality is locked.
knownIssues.workaround: Contact Varjo sales for enterprise licensing. For VR-only use, the standard license is sufficient.

knownIssues.title: Dual-cable setup — DisplayPort + USB-C both required
knownIssues.description: The XR-3 uses two cables from the headset to the PC. Both must be connected — neither can be omitted. Cable routing benefits from a ceiling-mounted pulley in active play.
knownIssues.workaround: Use the Varjo-supplied harness. Third-party cable extenders are not officially supported and can cause display dropouts.

optimizationTips.tip: Foveated rendering is essential at this resolution
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Without foveated rendering, the XR-3's combined resolution is extremely demanding. Calibrating eye tracking and enabling FR in Varjo Base is the single biggest performance win.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Varjo Base → Display → Foveated Rendering → Dynamic. At this resolution FR can reduce shading load by 40-50% with near-zero visual cost in motion.

optimizationTips.tip: Use Varjo's OpenXR runtime — not SteamVR — for supported titles
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Varjo's native OpenXR runtime skips SteamVR entirely and has lower latency plus direct access to foveated rendering features.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Set Varjo as the active OpenXR runtime via Varjo Base → General → Set as OpenXR runtime. Supported titles include MSFS, DCS, X-Plane, and many UE5 titles. Falling back to SteamVR for unsupported titles is fine.
